findings.title: Late cancel can mark a completed post-processing scan as cancelled
findings.evidence: memory-index.ts:1159 is the last scan-side cancellation check before post-processing; lines 1164-1357 can still run stale cleanup, backfills, invalidation, and record success, but handleMemoryIndexScan later chooses the terminal job state from the live cancel flag at memory-index.ts:1432. A cancel requested during any awaited post-processing step can therefore produce state='cancelled' with a completed result/status and completed side effects.
findings.recommendation: Derive the terminal job state from the scan response itself, e.g. only set 'cancelled' when runIndexScan returned the cancelled envelope, and add explicit cancellation checks inside/after awaited post-processing stages if cancellation is intended to stop those stages.
